questions.question: Which instituition, in association with Shiv Nadar University has developed a new technology to produce Lithium Sulphur Batteries?
questions.explanation: Researchers at IIT – Bombay and Shiv Nadar University have claimed that they have developed a technology to produce environment friendly Lithium-Sulfur (Li-S) batteries. The Li-S batteries would be three times more energy efficient and cost effective than conventional Lithium-ion batteries. The production of Li-S batteries uses products from the petroleum industry and agro-waste along with co polymers, and thus has a positive impact on environment.

questions.question: When did this year’s Earth Overshoot Day fall?
questions.explanation: This year, the Earth Overshoot Day fell on 22nd August, 2020. Earth Overshoot Day is the date when the demand for ecological resources and services by human beings exceeds what Earth can regenerate in a given year.This is computed by the think tank named Global Footprint Network using Earth’s Bio-capacity, Humanity’s Ecological Footprint among others.

questions.question: The COAS Unit Appreciation was conferred to the 51st Special Action Group of which Defence force?
questions.explanation: The COAS (Chief of Army Staff) Unit Appreciation was conferred to NSG’s (National Security Guard) 51 Special Action Group (SAG). The appreciation was conferred to the unit for the group’s exemplary achievements in combating terrorism. The most important operation undertaken by the unit is “Operation Black Tornado” which was launched against the terror strikes in Mumbai in November 2008.

questions.question: Dr.G.Satheesh Reddy who has been given a 2 year extension in the present position, is the Chief of which organization?
questions.explanation: Renowned scientist and the present head of Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) Dr.G.Satheesh Reddy has been given a 2 year extension as the Secretary, Department of Defence Research and Development and Chairman DRDO. This extension in his service was approved by the Appointments committee of the Union Cabinet. Earlier, he was appointed for the post in August 2018.

questions.question: Tsar’s bomb, that was seen in news recently, was developed by which country?
questions.explanation: Rosatom, the Russian nuclear energy agency, has recently released a documentary footage, titled ‘Top Secret: Test of a clean hydrogen bomb with a yield of 50 megatons’, that shows the detonation of the Tsar Bomba or Tsar’s bomb.It is claimed to be the world’s most powerful hydrogen bomb and it is over 3000 times as destructive as the bomb used in the Hiroshima attack. Tsar’s bomb was initially tested over Novaya Zemlya islands.

questions.question: What is the name of the interaction series launched by the Department of Science and Technology, to formulate STIP 2020?
questions.explanation: The Department of Science and Technology (DST) is launching a series of exclusive interactions with experts across the country named ‘In Conversation with’.This is a part of the process of the DST and the Office of the Principal Scientific Adviser (PSA) to the Government of India, in formulating the Science, Technology and Innovation Policy (STIP 2020). This is the fifth edition of the policy and will replace the existing policy, which was formulated in 2013.

questions.question: What is the enhanced borrowing limit permitted to states, in terms of their GSDP?
questions.explanation: The Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitaraman chaired the 41st GST Council meet, which discussed about the options that can be given to states to meet shortfall of GST compensation. Earlier in the month of May, the Centre amended the FRBM act to raise the borrowing limit of States from 3 per cent to 5 per cent of GSDP for FY21. After the meeting, the Finance Minister said, a further relaxation of 0.5 per cent in the limit will be given. This year, there is a GST compensation gap of 2.35 lakh crore rupees.

questions.question: As per the recent report released by McKinsey, at what rate India’s GDP should grow to create at least 90 million jobs?
questions.explanation: The McKinsey Global Institute has recently released a report titled ‘India’s turning point – An economic agenda to spur growth and jobs’.As per the report, it highlights that the country’s GDP, which is set to contract by over 5% in FY21 should grow at 8-8.5% per annum for the next decade to create at least 90 million new non-agricultural jobs required by 2030.

questions.question: Who is the longest serving Prime Minister (Premier) of Japan?
questions.explanation: Japan’s Prime Minister (Premier) Shinzo Abe, who is also the longest-serving premier, has resigned due to poor health.The term of the ruling party leader and Premier ends in September 2021. After his resignation, the Liberal Democratic Party (LDP) will select a nominee and the winner must be formally elected in Parliament. Abe is known for his famous economic strategy named ‘Abenomics’.

questions.question: Where is the headquarters of the Bureau of Police Research and Development (BPR &D) located?
questions.explanation: The Bureau of Police Research and Development, BPR&D is celebrating its Golden Jubilee Anniversary on August 28, 2020.To mark the day, a virtual event was organised with Union Minister of State for Home Affairs G Kishan Reddy as the Chief Guest. During the occasion, Central Detective Training Institute (CDTI), Jaipur and the website of Student Police Cadets were launched.
